title: SAP Senior Projekt Manager (w/m/d) für die Energiewirtschaft
required_skills: Multi-year SAP consulting experience in the energy sector (at least 50% SAP-focused)
Proven track record leading medium-to-large projects (>1,000 person-days)
Experience with offshore/nearshore resources and virtual team management
matched_skills: Project coordination and stakeholder management (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
Budget tracking and financial governance (HSBC: USD 8M programme)
missing_skills: SAP consulting or SAP product experience of any kind
Large-scale project leadership (>1,000 PT) track record
core_responsibilities: Plan all project activities including scheduling, resource planning and quality assurance
Manage financial aspects: cost planning, budget monitoring and project profitability
Execute project management using SAP methodology based on PMI
Coordinate with external parties (clients, partner providers)
Lead implementation team, coordinating SAP near/offshore centers and internal SAP units
Manage stakeholders across internal and external touchpoints
recommended_emphasis: HSBC: USD 8M budget governance with C-suite reporting — most relevant transferable skill for the financial management dimension
HSBC: 6-workstream cross-functional coordination — demonstrates programme orchestration capability
JIRA and Confluence as project management tools — partial relevance to SAP toolset context
summary: Boru scores 22.2 (poor match) for this senior SAP consulting role targeting energy sector specialists. The role explicitly requires multiple years of SAP consulting experience in energy — a domain entirely absent from Boru's CV. While programme coordination and budget governance skills transfer, the technical SAP depth and energy industry knowledge represent fundamental qualification gaps. Application is not recommended unless Boru can demonstrate adjacent SAP exposure not captured in the current CV.
